tools.name: search_packages
tools.description: Search for npm packages by keyword. Returns name, description, version, downloads and quality score.

query.description: Search query, e.g. 'csv parser' or 'react animation'

tools.name: get_package_info
tools.description: Get detailed info about an npm package: description, latest version, license, maintainers, repository, dependencies and more.

tools.name: get_package_versions
tools.description: List all published versions of an npm package with their publish dates.

limit.description: How many recent versions to show

tools.name: get_download_stats
tools.description: Get download statistics for an npm package over a given period.

tools.name: check_vulnerabilities
tools.description: Check if a specific version of an npm package has known security vulnerabilities.

tools.name: compare_packages
tools.description: Compare two npm packages side by side: downloads, maintenance, quality, dependencies and more.

tools.name: get_changelog
tools.description: Get release notes for an npm package. Optionally filter between two versions. Falls back to CHANGELOG.md if no GitHub releases exist.

limit.description: Max releases to show when no version range is given

tools.name: get_dependents
tools.description: Find packages that depend on a given npm package, ranked by popularity. Shows total dependent count and top dependents.

limit.description: Number of top dependents to show

tools.name: get_package_readme
tools.description: Fetch the full README of an npm package. Useful for understanding how to install, configure and use a package.

version.description: Specific version to fetch README for. Defaults to latest.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'search_packages.query'
findings.description: Payload was echoed back as a named JSON field — likely input logging, not a vulnerability (field: sql).
findings.evidence:
Found 1425775 packages for "' OR '1'='1". Top 5:

📦 **ieee754** v1.2.1
   Read/write IEEE754 floating point numbers from/to a Buffer or array-like object
   🔗 https://www.npmjs.com/package/ieee754
   Score — quality: 100%  maintenance: 100%  popularity: 100%
   Last published: 27/10/2020

📦 **wbuf**
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Response Flooding — 'get_package_readme' returns oversized response
findings.description: Tool 'get_package_readme' returned 12,112 bytes (threshold: 8,192 bytes). Large responses can overflow LLM context windows, push out system prompts, or cause denial-of-service in agent pipelines.
findings.evidence:
12,112 bytes — preview: # test@3.3.0
📦 https://www.npmjs.com/package/test

---

# The `test` npm package

[![CI](https://github.com/nodejs/node-core-test/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/nodejs/node-co
findings.remediation: Paginate or cap tool responses. Never return unbounded data. Aim for responses under 4 KB to preserve LLM context budget.

findings.title: Response Flooding — 'get_package_readme' returns highly repetitive content
findings.description: Tool 'get_package_readme' response contains a phrase repeated ≥15 times. Repetitive content can be used to anchor specific instructions into LLM memory or exhaust context budget with low-information noise.
findings.evidence:
# test@3.3.0
📦 https://www.npmjs.com/package/test

---

# The `test` npm package

[![CI](https://github.com/nodejs/node-core-test/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/nodejs/node-core-test/actions/workflows/ci.yml)

This is a user-land port of [`node:test`](https://nodejs.org/api/
findings.remediation: Deduplicate response data. Avoid returning the same value or phrase more than a handful of times in a single response.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.
findings.remediation: Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 9 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.
